nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 圣地亚哥首都大区
nodes.summary: 圣地亚哥首都大区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括Slender-billed parakeet、Straight-billed earthcreeper，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Phillip Parker King
nodes.summary: Phillip Parker King是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括Imperial shag、Slender-billed parakeet，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: 艾森大区
nodes.summary: 艾森大区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联9种鸟类，包括智利斑䳍、Chilean pigeon、Slender-billed parakeet等9种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Slender-billed parakeet
nodes.summary: Slender-billed parakeet（Slender-billed parakeet，学名 Enicognathus leptorhynchus），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括森林砍伐。

nodes.name: 湖大区
nodes.summary: 湖大区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联3种鸟类，包括智利斑䳍、Slender-billed parakeet、斑眼霸鹟，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: |year=2022 |title=Enicognathus leptorhynchus |volume=2022 |article-number=e.T22685891A208980485 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2022-1.RLTS.T22685891A208980485.en |access-date=15 March 2023}} | status2 = CITES_A2 | status2_system = CITES | status2_ref = | genus = Enicognathus | species = leptorhynchus | authority = (King, 1831) | range_map = Enicognathus leptorhynchus map.svg | range_map_caption = Approximate range in green | synonyms = Enicognathus byroni }} The slender-billed parakeet (Enicognathus leptorhynchus) or choroy, is a species of bird in subfamily Arinae of the family Psittacidae, the African and New World parrots

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The slender-billed parakeet is endemic to central Chile between the Santiago Metropolitan Region and Los Lagos Region; it also occurs in the Aysén Region, but rarely, and has been recorded as a vagrant in Argentina.Remsen, J

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The slender-billed parakeet is endemic to central Chile between the Santiago Metropolitan Region and Los Lagos Region; it also occurs in the Aysén Region, but rarely, and has been recorded as a vagrant in Argentina.Remsen, J

links.label: 分布于

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: No immediate threats have been identified. Though its population declined in the 1950s and 1960s due to deforestation and other causes, it is still considered fairly common
